question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicService.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the EntityId.of(String) parsing — which uses filter(n -> n >= 0) to silently drop negative parsed values — to craft a string like '0.-1.5' where the negative realm is filtered out, resulting in a parts.size() != 3 check failure that throws IllegalArgumentException with the full input string in the message, leaking the raw user input?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicService.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the TopicController's sequential calls to topicService.findById(), entityService.findById(), and customFeeService.findById() — where findById() throws EntityNotFoundException if the topic is missing — to determine whether a given ID exists as a topic vs. as another entity type by comparing which service throws the exception first?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicService.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findById() returns a mutable Topic domain object (annotated with @Data which generates setters) that, if shared across requests due to caching, could be mutated by one request handler and affect data returned to another user?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicService.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the EntityId.of(long id) static cache — which is a JVM-level singleton — to cause cross-tenant data leakage in a multi-tenant deployment where different tenants share the same JVM instance and the same EntityId cache?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicService.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findById() does not validate the EntityId against a whitelist of known valid topic ID ranges, allowing lookup of topic IDs in reserved or system-level ranges (e.g., 0.0.1 through 0.0.100) that may expose sensitive system topic metadata?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicService.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the EntityId.encode() method's bit-masking — where (num & NUM_MASK) truncates num to 38 bits — to craft a num value that, after masking, collides with a different topic's encoded ID, causing findById() to return data for the wrong topic?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicService.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of CORS restrictions on the GET /api/v1/topics/{id} endpoint (Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *) to perform cross-origin topic data exfiltration from a malicious web page, harvesting topic metadata from authenticated users' browsers?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicService.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findById() does not implement circuit-breaker or bulkhead patterns, causing a slow database response to block all threads in the service's thread pool and create a cascading failure that takes down the entire REST API?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicService.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the EntityId.of(String) method's use of Objects.requireNonNullElse(entityId, '') — which converts null to empty string — combined with the subsequent parts.size() != 3 check, to cause an IllegalArgumentException that includes the literal string 'null' in the error message, confirming null-handling behavior to the attacker?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicService.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findById() does not implement any result-set size validation on the Topic object returned by topicRepository.findById(), allowing a database record with abnormally large field values (e.g., a very long memo field) to cause excessive memory allocation during deserialization?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicService.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the EntityId.of(long id) cache — which uses Caffeine with CACHE_SPEC read from system property HIERO_MIRROR_COMMON_CACHE_ENTITYID — to determine if the system property is overridden to a smaller cache size, enabling more efficient cache eviction attacks?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicService.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findById() does not validate that the EntityId's num component is non-zero before querying the database, allowing lookup of topic ID 0.0.0 which maps to EntityId.EMPTY, potentially returning an unintended database record?
